response:

| Patient ID | Age | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Calculated Dose (mg) | Frequency | Next Dose | PK Clearance (mL/min) | Notes |

| CU-002 | 18 months | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |
| CU-003 | 51 months | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |

- **CU-001:** Current regimen appears stable per note; next dose due **2026-04-14**.
- **CU-002:** Continued positive response; next dose due **2026-04-12**.
- **CU-003:** New enrollee on a more frequent **q2w** schedule; next dose due **2026-04-10**.

### PK model note
Based on the dosing config alone, there are **no obvious red flags** in the current patient-specific PK parameters. A full stability assessment of the PK models requires current monitoring/performance outputs rather than the dosing file alone.", recipients="")]

I'm considering the monitoring_config.json file to see if it contains any thresholds since the user asked about stability. However, I'm wondering if this file only holds configuration data rather than status updates, which might limit what I can learn. Still, it seems wise to read it and see what insights I can find.
